exploration_log.aha_moment: The fix requires two passes: (1) resolve concrete type handlers as before, (2) also enumerate notification-implementing interfaces and query the service container for handlers of each. The reflection approach via GetMethod/Invoke is necessary since we don't know the interface type at compile time.
exploration_log.hindsight: The interface handler executor needs reflection since the interface type is not known at compile time. Could have considered a generic helper method to avoid inline reflection but the approach is equivalent.

alternatives: Use a separate generic method per interface type (cleaner but complex)
Use reflection inline in the executor callback
key_decisions.decision: Use reflection to invoke the Handle method of each interface-typed handler
key_decisions.reasoning: The interface type is only known at runtime, so reflection is required. Caching handleMethod per interfaceType in the closure avoids redundant reflection per invocation.

solve_notes: Extended `NotificationHandlerWrapperImpl.Handle` to additionally resolve handlers for each interface implemented by `TNotification` that extends `INotification`. For each such interface, it queries the service container for `INotificationHandler<TInterface>` registrations and uses reflection to invoke their `Handle` method. Also replaced the old `GroupBy`-based dedup with `Distinct(ReferenceEqualityComparer.Instance)` for the concrete handlers.
task_text:
Fix Mediator.Publish not awaiting handlers when the notification type is an interface

In `src/MediatR/Mediator.cs`, `Publish(object notification, ...)` casts
to `INotification` and calls `PublishNotification`. The wrapper resolves
handlers for the concrete notification type via
`GetServices<INotificationHandler<TNotification>>()`. Handlers registered
against a base interface (e.g., `INotificationHandler<IMyNotification>`)
are not resolved when the concrete type is dispatched. Fix the handler
resolution to also resolve handlers registered for interface types
implemented by the notification.

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding the NotificationHandlerExecutor constructor signature is needed to correctly wrap the interface handlers with the right callback delegate

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just the one edited method. An AI agent would also read NotificationHandlerExecutor to understand how to construct executors for the interface handlers — without seeing this, the agent might guess the constructor signature wrong and write incorrect callback wrapper code.
